meta.purpose: Real-time truck blind spot monitoring dashboard. A Python/YOLO backend detects objects around a truck via cameras, publishes to Kafka, a Node.js bridge streams them over WebSocket, and this React frontend visualises detections as a live 3D scene with alert system.
meta.renderer: Plain Three.js (import * as THREE from 'three') — NO @react-three/fiber, NO @react-three/drei, NO OrbitControls import. Manual orbit via mouse events implemented in useThreeScene hook.
meta.font: AeonikProTRIAL — loaded from local .otf files (Regular 400, Bold 700, Light 300). Falls back to system sans-serif.
meta.websocket: ws://{serverIP}:8081 — receives JSON messages with a detections array

hooksArchitecture.useWebSocket: src/hooks/useWebSocket.js — manages WS lifecycle, auto-reconnect 3 s, FPS counter via setInterval
hooksArchitecture.useThreeScene: src/hooks/useThreeScene.js — full Three.js scene setup (renderer, camera, lights, truck mesh, idle/active zones, orbit controls, animation loop)

designLanguage.theme: Split: Header + side panel = white/off-white. 3D scene canvas = light gray (#f0f0f0). The dark CSS token variables exist but are NOT applied to the main UI surfaces.
designLanguage.colorPhilosophy: One accent rule: #CC2222 red is used EXCLUSIVELY for danger/alert states. Everything else is white, light-gray, or near-black text.
designLanguage.glassEffect: NONE — all surfaces are opaque solid colors.
designLanguage.animations: Minimal — only functional state transitions (hover 200 ms, alert slide-in from top). No looping animations.
designLanguage.borders: 1px solid #e2e2e2 separates all light-theme sections and cards.
designLanguage.borderRadius: Cards 8px, buttons 6px, pill for camera group selector.

colorTokens.note: Dark tokens (--bg-0 through --bg-4) are defined in variables.css but the main UI surfaces override them with explicit light values.
colorTokens.sceneBackground: #f0f0f0 — Three.js renderer clear color AND .app__scene CSS background

stackOrder: 1. Header (.hd) via .app__header-wrap — flex-shrink:0; position:relative (alert banner anchored here)
2. Alert banner (.alert-banner) — position:absolute top:0 left:0 right:0 inside header-wrap; slides down from translateY(-100%) to translateY(0)
3. Main body (.app__body) — flex:1; display:flex; flex-direction:row; overflow:hidden
│     └── view buttons (.scene-views) — position:absolute bottom:14px left:50% translateX(-50%)
└── Side panel (.sp via DetectionPanel) — fixed 290px width; background:#f5f5f5

Header.layout: flex row, space-between, align-center, gap 16px, padding 0 20px

right__actions.content: connection indicator (.hd__conn — dot + label) + icon buttons (.hd__icon-btn) for reconnect and settings

ThreeDScene.implementation: src/hooks/useThreeScene.js — initialised via useRef canvas element in App.js
ThreeDScene.library: Plain Three.js (import * as THREE from 'three') — NO fiber/drei
lighting: AmbientLight(0xffffff, 0.85) — soft fill, no color tint
DirectionalLight(0xffffff, 1.6) position(8,12,6) — key light, no color tint
ThreeDScene.truckMesh: BoxGeometry primitives composed in a group — cab + chassis + roof

zones.blind: left, right, rear — idle edge 0xcc4444 @ opacity:0.25; active fill 0xcc4444 @ 0.20, edge @ 0.85
zones.front: non-blind — idle edge 0x999999 @ opacity:0.18; active fill 0x333333 @ 0.06, edge @ 0.40

ThreeDScene.orbit: Manual — mousedown/mousemove/mouseup/wheel event listeners on canvas; spherical coords; damping

DetectionPanel.position: Right side panel — fixed 290px width inside .app__body flex row

tabs.tabStyle: color:#aaaaaa inactive; active: color:#0a0a0a border-bottom:2px solid #0a0a0a

DetectionPanel.footer: .sp__foot — background:#ffffff; border-top:#e2e2e2; shows stat summary (avgConf, count)

sections.header: object type label (.dc__type color:#0a0a0a) + confidence badge (.dc__conf bg:#f2f2f2 border:#e0e0e0 color:#666666)
sections.meta: zone dot (.dc__zone-dot — #bbbbbb normal, var(--red) blind) + zone text + timestamp (.dc__time live-updating, shows 'now' when <2 s)

AlertBanner.position: position:absolute inside .app__header-wrap; slides down from top when active
AlertBanner.trigger: any detection where camera_zone is left | right | rear

SettingsModal.triggeredBy: settings icon button in Header right area
SettingsModal.content: WebSocket server IP input + apply button

dataFlow.hook: useWebSocket(serverIP) in App.js — returns { detections, connected, fps, reconnect }
dataFlow.alertBehavior: alert-banner slides down for 3 s then retracts
dataFlow.fps: message count per second tracked by useWebSocket setInterval

fileStructure.src/App.js: Root — state, layout, useWebSocket + useThreeScene wiring
fileStructure.src/hooks/useThreeScene.js: Three.js scene hook — renderer, lights, truck, zones, orbit, animation loop
fileStructure.src/components/ui/: Alert, Badge, Button, Card, Modal, StatusIndicator, StatWidget (each .js + .css)
fileStructure.src/components/Truck3D.js: Legacy — truck mesh now built inside useThreeScene.js directly

knownInconsistencies: Truck3D.js and DetectionOverlay.js exist as separate files but the active scene is fully driven by useThreeScene.js — those old component files may be unused stubs
Dark --bg-* CSS tokens remain in variables.css but the main UI surfaces use explicit light values (#ffffff, #f5f5f5, #f0f0f0) — tokens are not wired to the white theme yet
